title: Automated decisions based on descriptive models
abstract: In one embodiment, a method includes accessing input data from one or more different input sources. The input sources include: one or more cameras, one or more microphones, and a social graph maintained by a social-networking system. Based on the input data, generating a current descriptive model for a current audio-video communication session that comprises one or more descriptive characteristics about (1) an environment associated with the current audio- video communication session, (2) one or more people within the environment, or (3) one or more contextual elements associated with the current audio-video communication session. The method also includes generating one or more instructions for the current audio-video communication session that are based the one or more descriptive characteristics; and sending the one or more instructions to a computing device associated with the one or more cameras and the one or more microphones.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising, by a computing device: at a first time during an audio-video communication session: determining that a first participant is located in an environment associated with the audio-video communication session; locating a first body region of the first participant; generating a first color histogram of the first body region, wherein the first color histogram represents a first distribution of one or more colors of the first body region; at a second time during the audio-video communication: determining that a second participant is located in the environment; locating a second body region of the second participant that is the same as the first body region; generating a second color histogram of the second body region, wherein the second color histogram represents a second distribution of one or more colors of the second body region; determining that the first color histogram is the same as the second color histogram; based on the determination that the first color histogram is the same as the second color histogram, determining that the first participant is the same as the second participant. 2. The method of Claim 1, wherein the first color histogram and the second color histogram each comprise a number of pixel buckets, wherein the pixel buckets comprise pixels whose color value is within a span associated with the respective pixel bucket. 3. The method of Claim 1, further comprising identifying the first participant and storing the first color histogram in a social graph associated with the first participant. 4. The method of Claim 1, further comprising determining one or more body ratios for the first participant and the second participant, the body ratios comprising a shoulder-to-torso 

 ratio or a head-to-torso ratio, wherein the determining that the first participant is the same as the second participant is further based on the one or more body ratios. 5. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: determining a first current location for the first participant and a first trajectory for the first participant; determining a second current location for the second participant and a second trajectory for the second participant, wherein the determining that the first participant is the same as the second participant is further based on the first location, first trajectory, second location, and second trajectory. 6. The method of Claim 5, further comprising assigning a plurality of weights to the first trajectory, second trajectory, first location, second location, first color histogram, and second color histogram, wherein at a first weight of the plurality of weights is greater than a second weight of the plurality of weights. 7. The method of Claim 1, wherein the first body region and the second body region are a torso region. 8. One or more computer-readable non-transitory storage media embodying software that is operable when executed to: at a first time during an audio-video communication session: determine that a first participant is located in an environment associated with the audio-video communication session; locate a first body region of the first participant; generate a first color histogram of the first body region, wherein the first color histogram represents a first distribution of one or more colors of the first body region; at a second time during the audio-video communication: determine that a second participant is located in the environment; locate a second body region of the second participant that is the same as the first body region; 

 generate a second color histogram of the second body region, wherein the second color histogram represents a second distribution of one or more colors of the second body region; determine that the first color histogram is the same as the second color histogram; based on the determination that the first color histogram is the same as the second color histogram, determining that the first participant is the same as the second participant. 9. The media of Claim 8, wherein the first color histogram and the second color histogram each comprise a number of pixel buckets, wherein the pixel buckets comprise pixels whose color value is within a span associated with the respective pixel bucket. 10. The media of Claim 8, wherein the software is further operable when executed to identify the first participant and storing the first color histogram in a social graph associated with the first participant. 11. The media of Claim 8, wherein the software is further operable when executed to determine one or more body ratios for the first participant and the second participant, the body ratios comprising a shoulder-to-torso ratio or a head-to-torso ratio, wherein the determining that the first participant is the same as the second participant is further based on the one or more body ratios. 12. The media of Claim 8, wherein the software is further operable when executed to: determine a first current location for the first participant and a first trajectory for the first participant; and determine a second current location for the second participant and a second trajectory for the second participant, wherein the determining that the first participant is the same as the second participant is further based on the first location, first trajectory, second location, and second trajectory. 13. The media of Claim 8, wherein the software is further operable when executed to assign a plurality of weights to the first trajectory, second trajectory, first location, second location, first color histogram, and second color histogram, wherein at a first weight of the plurality of weights is greater than a second weight of the plurality of weights. 14. The media of Claim 8, wherein the first body region and the second body region are a torso region. 15.
